description: We commit to Mercury: not as conquest, but as the first industrial foothold close enough to the Sun to change what civilisation can build.
outcomeSummary: Opens the Mercury mission chain.
Unlocks early Moon research tracks.
Begins the path toward solar-scale industry.

description: We stitch solar, wind, storage, and grid forecasting into a calmer planetary metabolism, making clean power ordinary enough to build from.
outcomeSummary: Enables Deuterium Extraction From Seawater.
Enables Automated Food Systems and Earth branch choices.
Provides a stable early research foundation.

description: We learn how to make sealed worlds feel less like capsules and more like places where ordinary life can take root.
outcomeSummary: Supports future Mars dome and aerogel habitat options.
Improves early off-world living before open atmospheres are ready.
Creates a practical bridge between Earth cities and colony habitats.

description: We turn the oceans into a patient fuel source, separating rare hydrogen from abundance without treating abundance as permission to waste.
outcomeSummary: Enables Fusion Ignition Theory.
Connects Earth's clean power base to future Type 1 energy systems.
Prepares civilisation for deuterium-scale industry.

description: We stop treating the ocean as a place where mistakes disappear. Fleets, microbes, and law begin pulling a century of fragments home.
outcomeSummary: Becomes available after the Mercury mission begins.
Strengthens Earth's Naturalist recovery arc.
Improves the baseline for later ocean restoration work.

description: We push controlled fusion from a promise into an engineering language, one careful confinement model at a time.
outcomeSummary: Triggers a landmark culture event when completed.
Marks the theoretical step toward Type 1 civilisation.
Enables late Architect research paths that depend on fusion.

description: Venus teaches us carbonate patience at planetary scale. We bring that lesson home and begin lowering the fever without pretending it was simple.
outcomeSummary: Unlocked by Venus carbonate process spillover.
Enables Ocean Acidification Reversal.
Advances Earth's long Naturalist recovery path.

description: Mars makes us humble about dirt. We relearn soil as a living fabric and coordinate repair from farms to abandoned industrial ground.
outcomeSummary: Unlocked by Mars soil-study spillover.
Enables Rewilding At Scale with Mars coastal stability.
Improves Earth's ecological recovery arc.

description: We give the reefs, shell-builders, and plankton time to breathe again, correcting chemistry at a pace the living ocean can survive.
outcomeSummary: Requires CO2 Drawdown and Venus carbonate spillover.
Deepens Earth's ocean restoration branch.
Supports the broader Naturalist recovery endpoint.
unlockCondition: Complete Atmospheric CO2 Drawdown. Requires venus carbonate process started.

description: We return room to rivers, forests, pollinators, and migration. The work is not nostalgia; it is infrastructure made of living systems.
outcomeSummary: Adds a Naturalist branch decision tag.
Requires Earth soil restoration and Mars coastal stability.
Represents a major Earth recovery milestone.
unlockCondition: Complete Soil Restoration Network. Requires mars coastal stable.

description: We make food production precise enough to free land and labour, then face the harder question of what that freedom is for.
outcomeSummary: Presents a fork between Naturalist rewilding and Architect development.
Can trigger a culture event through the chosen fork outcome.
The chosen branch sets the tone for later Earth identity research.

description: We build upward with discipline, concentrating people, services, and transit so a city can grow without endlessly widening its wound.
outcomeSummary: Adds an Architect branch decision tag.
Concentrates housing and services without widening the city footprint.
Triggers the first 50-million megacity culture event.

description: We design cities as complete habitats: dense, efficient, and humane enough that scale no longer has to mean sprawl.
outcomeSummary: Adds an Architect branch decision tag.
Applies the dense-living colonist bonus.
Enables deeper Architect city and resource-grid research.

description: We learn to live below the noise of weather and traffic, turning depth into shelter rather than exile.
outcomeSummary: Adds an Architect branch decision tag.
Triggers an underground-communities culture event.
Transfers design confidence to protected off-world habitats.

description: We connect supply, need, weather, harvest, transit, and waste into one visible system, then use that visibility to prevent suffering early.
outcomeSummary: Adds an Architect branch decision tag.
Makes supply risks visible early enough for coordinated response.
Triggers a culture event about famine prevented before it starts.

description: We coordinate climate intervention with public memory and restraint, learning to manage an atmosphere without forgetting it is shared.
outcomeSummary: Adds an Architect branch decision tag.
Improves the narrative bridge between Earth management and terraforming.
Enables more confident late planetary coordination.

description: We study the boundary between mind and system carefully, because a civilisation that thinks faster still has to decide wisely.
outcomeSummary: Adds an Architect branch decision tag.
Frames the debate over human judgment inside planetary systems.
Triggers a major culture event about human-system integration.
unlockCondition: Complete Planetary Resource Grid and Fusion Ignition Theory.

description: We let the planet's logistics run with a steadier intelligence, then ask what humans choose when survival is no longer the daily assignment.
outcomeSummary: Adds an Architect branch decision tag.
Coordinates planetary logistics as a mature civic system.
Triggers a culture event about life after planetary automation.

description: We study bodies that grow, heal, and age under one-sixth gravity, learning what care means when even weight is different.
outcomeSummary: Opens later Radiation Resilience research.
Improves the medical basis for off-world settlement.
Supports healthier future Mars colonists.

description: We make small habitats patient and forgiving, cycling air, water, food, and waste until survival becomes routine rather than heroic.
outcomeSummary: Opens Isolation Psychology research.
Improves sealed habitat reliability.
Supports future dome and sky-city living.

description: We turn dust into shelter, learning to print, sinter, shield, and repair with the ground already under our feet.
outcomeSummary: Opens Early He-3 Extraction research.
Improves local construction knowledge for off-world bases.
Reduces dependence on Earth-shipped materials in future planning.

description: We catalogue hardy pioneer life in controlled lunar systems, building a living library for worlds that are not ready yet.
outcomeSummary: Provides early biological candidates for terraforming.
Supports Mars Biological Seeding prerequisites.
Opens Tier 2 once Mars confirms open water.

description: We learn how cells, materials, and communities endure the invisible weather beyond Earth's magnetic shelter.
outcomeSummary: Builds on Low-Gravity Medicine.
Improves long-duration crew safety in exposed habitats.
Transfers protective knowledge to Mars and Mercury planning.

description: We study the rituals, spaces, and habits that keep small crews kind when every disagreement has nowhere to go.
outcomeSummary: Builds on Closed-Loop Life Support.
Improves social resilience for future colonies.
Supports long missions where evacuation is not simple.

description: We test the slow chemistry of pulling rare isotopes from ordinary dust, bridging lunar industry to the fusion era ahead.
outcomeSummary: Builds on Regolith Construction.
Adds a lunar bridge toward fusion-era resource handling.
Strengthens the Moon's role as research outpost and proving ground.

description: With Martian water no longer theoretical, we broaden the library from survival specialists into early ecosystems.
outcomeSummary: Requires Tier 1 and Mars ocean spillover.
Improves biological options for later Mars and Venus seeding.
Turns Moon research into a living terraforming catalogue.
unlockCondition: Complete Organism Library Tier 1. Requires mars ocean confirmed.

description: We choose the bluntest warming path: fast, irreversible, and impossible to pretend is only engineering.
outcomeSummary: Applies the Mars polar detonation terraforming choice.
Adds an Architect branch decision tag.
Triggers the Mars polar detonation culture event.

description: We place patient light above Mars, warming the planet by aim and persistence rather than shock.
outcomeSummary: Applies the Mars orbital mirrors terraforming choice.
Adds an Architect branch decision tag.
Provides a reusable warming infrastructure path.

description: We shelter Mars from the solar wind, not by remaking its core, but by holding a protective field where the planet needs it most.
outcomeSummary: Applies the Mars magnetic umbrella terraforming choice.
Adds a Naturalist branch decision tag.
Enables Comet Water Delivery.

description: We make small warm places under thin light, letting Mars host pockets of pressure and life before the whole sky is ready.
outcomeSummary: Requires Launch Mercury Mission and Earth Dome Habitat Technology.
Applies the Mars aerogel domes terraforming choice.
Creates a gentler bridge toward open Martian habitats.
unlockCondition: Requires Launch Mercury Mission and earth dome habitat.

description: We redirect distant ice toward a dry world, turning celestial bookkeeping into rain that will not arrive for decades.
outcomeSummary: Applies the Mars comet water delivery choice.
Requires Magnetic Umbrella protection.
Enables Mars Ocean Confirmed.
unlockCondition: Complete Magnetic Umbrella. Requires Launch Mercury Mission.

description: For the first time, Martian water is no longer a mineral memory or sealed tank. It gathers under open sky.
outcomeSummary: Triggers the first liquid water culture event.
Spillover unlocks Moon Organism Library Tier 2.
Enables Biological Seeding.

transfersFrom.reason: Earth ocean repair gives us a baseline for interpreting water chemistry elsewhere.
transfersFrom.description: Future life-search outcomes can resolve more strongly when Earth ocean repair is complete.

description: We introduce life as a promise with safeguards, choosing organisms humble enough to begin and resilient enough to matter.
outcomeSummary: Applies the Mars biological seeding choice.
Adds a Naturalist branch decision tag.
Enables Mars Soil Study Complete.
unlockCondition: Complete Mars Ocean Confirmed. Requires moon organism library t1.

transfersFrom.reason: Closed habitats teach us how ecologies fail slowly enough to recover.
transfersFrom.description: Mars biological seeding has steadier outcomes after closed-loop life support.

description: The first Martian soils become readable: chemistry, microbes, failure, promise, and the slow grammar of a living ground.
outcomeSummary: Spillover unlocks Earth's Soil Restoration Network.
Enables Mars Coastal Ecosystems Stable.
Turns Mars ecology into useful Earth restoration knowledge.

description: The coasts hold through seasons. Not paradise, not yet, but stable enough that life can start making its own weather of habit.
outcomeSummary: Spillover unlocks Earth's Rewilding At Scale.
Marks a major Mars biosphere stability step.
Closes the early Mars ecological research chain.

description: We begin by giving Venus shade, placing a deliberate dusk between the planet and the furnace that made it unlivable.
outcomeSummary: Applies the Venus orbital shade mirror choice.
Adds an Architect branch decision tag.
Triggers the Venus cooling culture event.

description: We turn atmosphere into stone carefully, lowering pressure through chemistry rather than pretending Venus can be hurried safely.
outcomeSummary: Applies the Venus carbonate sequestration choice.
Adds a Naturalist branch decision tag.
Enables Venus Carbonate Process Started.

transfersFrom.reason: Earth-scale drawdown teaches us how to move carbon without pretending atmosphere is simple.
transfersFrom.description: Venus CO2 removal planning can reuse Earth drawdown logistics.

description: Venus begins teaching Earth in return. The same chemistry that quiets one atmosphere can help repair another.
outcomeSummary: Spillover unlocks Earth's Atmospheric CO2 Drawdown.
Spillover unlocks Earth's Ocean Acidification Reversal.
Marks the beginning of Venus-to-Earth climate knowledge transfer.

transfersFrom.reason: Earth carbon capture gives Venus an operational vocabulary for carbonate sequestration.
transfersFrom.description: Venus carbonate work is informed by completed Earth CO2 Drawdown.

description: We commit to a terrible precision: using a moon's ice and momentum to remake Venus, knowing the decision will echo for centuries.
outcomeSummary: Applies the Venus Europa impact choice permanently.
Adds an Architect branch decision tag.
Represents the high-commitment ocean-and-spin Venus path.

description: Before the surface can welcome us, the clouds can hold us. We design cities that float where pressure and temperature briefly become kind.
outcomeSummary: Applies the Venus sky cities choice.
Adds a Naturalist branch decision tag.
Enables Sky City Descent.

description: As Venus changes beneath them, the cities must descend, adapt, or surrender buoyancy to a world becoming less hostile.
outcomeSummary: Applies the Venus sky city descent choice.
Adds an Architect branch decision tag.
Moves the sky-city path toward long-term surface transition.
